segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: And President Trump loves to talk about his TV ratings, if not his current approval rating.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: So he must be pleased to know that 33 million people watched the prime time roll out of his

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: But what do Americans think of the choice?

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: Well, a brand new CNN poll shows that a plurality, 39%, were left with a positive first impression

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: that compares with 45% for President Obama's never confirmed pick, Judge Marik Garland,

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: and 54% for now chief justice, John Roberts.

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: But when it comes to confirming the new nominee, opinions are sharply divided along party

segments.speaker: SPEAKER_00
segments.text: Just 22 percent of Democrats think the Senate should vote to confirm him while an overwhelming
